name: Brucellosis
description: Canine brucellosis is a bacterial reproductive disease caused by Brucella canis. It causes reproductive failure in breeding dogs, including abortion, stillbirths, and infertility, as well as orchitis in males. It is a zoonotic disease transmissible to humans.
treatment_overview: Treatment with prolonged antibiotic courses (doxycycline combined with aminoglycosides) reduces bacterial load but rarely achieves complete cure. Neutering reduces transmission risk. Infected dogs should not be bred.
prevention_tips: Test all breeding dogs before mating with RSAT or AGID test
New dogs entering a kennel should be tested and quarantined
Maintain closed or tested breeding programmes
